items.title: Streamlined Claude Code Documentation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update consolidates 11 articles in the Claude Code + MCP series into a unified template, enhancing clarity and reducing redundancy. Key changes include the removal of repetitive sections and the verification of all cross-article links to ensure they point to valid documents. The overall document size has been reduced by 178 lines, improving accessibility for users.

items.title: Enhance Payment Flows for AliPay and WeChat in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces two key improvements to the payment flow in Nexior. First, AliPay now correctly passes a 'surface' hint for mobile users, ensuring a seamless experience. Second, WeChat Pay has been enhanced for mobile users outside of WeChat, providing a Native QR code and guidance for payment completion.

items.title: Fix sleep unit confusion across all workers in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses the confusion between seconds and milliseconds in the sleep function used across 12 workers. Key changes include standardizing the sleep function to ensure consistent timing and adding comments to clarify the expected units. This fix reduces latency and prevents future errors related to incorrect time delays.

items.title: Fix millisecond polling in ephone workers in Platform Service ⚡
items.summary: Corrected the `sleep` calls in `seedance/videos.ts` and `udio/audios.ts` to use milliseconds instead of seconds. This change restores the intended polling intervals to 5 seconds, preventing excessive requests and CPU usage. The update aligns ephone pollers with other handlers and resolves the inadvertent high-frequency polling issue.

items.title: Enhance Console Usage List Experience in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update aligns the Console usage list in Nexior with the PlatformFrontend, ensuring a consistent filter, export, and detail experience for API usage records. Key features include a new HTTP status-code filter, an Export CSV button, and additional columns for elapsed time and balance after deduction. The changes also enhance the user interface with a detail dialog for each record, improving overall usability.

items.title: Fix polling interval and task response handling in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the polling interval for flux jobs by changing the unit from milliseconds to seconds, ensuring timely job responses. A placeholder response is now returned during the polling period, improving client feedback and preventing confusion. Additionally, logging enhancements have been added for better traceability of job statuses.

items.title: Enhance Checkout Experience with Anonymous Payment Page in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces an anonymous order payment page at `/orders/:id`, allowing users to complete transactions without logging in. Key features include support for WeChat, Stripe, and AliPay, with appropriate state alerts for free, paid, failed, and expired orders. The implementation mirrors the existing functionality on PlatformFrontend, ensuring a seamless user experience.

items.title: Align Console Order Detail with PlatformFrontend in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the Nexior Console order detail page by aligning its visual presentation with the PlatformFrontend. Key changes include a tag-styled display for payment methods and the addition of a credits amount row, ensuring a consistent user experience across platforms while preserving existing Nexior-specific functionalities.

items.title: Streamlined Integration Guides for Claude Code in Backend 🚀
items.summary: The Claude Code documentation has been refined to feature only five essential integration guides: VS Code, Cursor, Terminal (CLI), JetBrains, and GitHub Actions. The 5-minute quickstart and Desktop integration articles have been removed to enhance clarity and focus. This update ensures that users have direct access to the most relevant resources for effective integration.

items.title: Add K8s Deployment and Service for Flux in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new Kubernetes Deployment and Service for the Flux API, ensuring that the `/flux/images` endpoint is properly routed. The deployment includes two replicas and is configured similarly to existing handlers, providing a stable upstream for Kong. Additionally, the rollout script has been updated to include the new configurations, streamlining the deployment process.

items.title: Order list gets summary cards and CSV export in Nexior 📊
items.summary: The order list page now includes four summary cards for total orders, total spent, finished orders, and pending orders, powered by the new orders summary endpoint. This update also adds state, payment method, and date-range filters with quick-select shortcuts, plus CSV export for downloading filtered results. Supporting OrderService methods, icon registration, and new order-related i18n keys were added across all locales to keep the experience consistent with PlatformFrontend.

items.title: Update Flux Models and Workflows in Dify 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the deprecated `flux-pro-1.1` and `flux-pro-1.1-ultra` models with the new Flux 2 family in the Dify tool plugin. All references in the workflow templates have been updated to ensure a smooth transition for existing users, preventing unexpected price changes. The final model lineup now includes seven options, enhancing the flexibility and capabilities of the tool.

items.title: Updated Flux model support for TTApi migration in M C Ps 🚀
items.summary: Aligned the Flux model lineup with the TTApi backend by removing legacy flux-pro-1.1 variants and adding the Flux 2 family. Updated the core model type definitions, documentation, and model-selection guidance to reflect the new supported models. This keeps MCP configuration and examples consistent with the available backend offerings and avoids references to deprecated SKUs.

items.title: Refresh Flux Model Options for TTApi Migration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Updated the Flux model selector to align with the TTApi backend by removing the legacy flux-pro-1.1 variants and adding the Flux 2 family: flux-2-flex, flux-2-pro, and flux-2-max. The available lineup now reflects upstream-supported models while keeping flux-dev as the default. This ensures the UI matches current backend capabilities and avoids offering unsupported model options.

items.title: Revamp of Flux Images API with New TTApi Models in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update realigns the public Flux Images API to the new TTApi, introducing a lineup of seven models with updated pricing. The endpoint structure remains unchanged, but the model enum and pricing have been revised to reflect the new offerings. Comprehensive tests have been conducted to ensure functionality and performance.

items.title: Launch TTApi Flux Image Worker and Refresh Model Support in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: Added a new TTApi-backed /flux/images worker that supports both image generation and editing while preserving the existing response contract for downstream clients. The implementation handles job submission, completion polling, COS uploads, and public URL delivery, and introduces support for seven Flux models including the new Flux 2 lineup. Legacy and unsupported models were removed, and configuration, routing, and settings were updated to enable the new upstream integration. Local validation passed across generate and edit smoke tests.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Pay flow with QR screenshot guidance and public link fallback in Frontend Web ⚡
items.summary: This update fixes two mobile WeChat Pay issues: missing QR codes on iPhone Safari and order pages showing empty after re-login. It adds a screenshot-and-scan guide for mobile users outside WeChat, updates the copy-link to use a public `/orders/:id` route, and ensures the QR dialog fits properly inside the WeChat in-app browser. Multilingual tips for the new mobile flow were also added to i18n files.

items.title: Fix iOS App Store version lookup and soft warnings in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the iOS App Store version lookup by filtering directly on versionString instead of using an unsupported sort field, preventing 400 errors during submission. It also introduces consistent soft warnings for non-2xx responses and fixes the 409 fallback filter for review submissions. Only the Submit step was modified; other TestFlight processing and distribution steps remain unchanged. YAML validation passed, ensuring pipeline stability for future runs.

items.title: Add Subsite Deletion Feature in Nexior 🔥
items.summary: This update introduces a new delete action for subsites in the settings menu, allowing users to easily remove subsites directly from the interface. Previously, deletion required backend access, but now users can delete a subsite with a simple click, enhancing usability. The feature includes confirmation prompts and localized messages for a seamless experience.

items.title: Unblock Mobile Release Retries with Dedicated Distribution Jobs in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: The mobile release workflows were updated to separate iOS TestFlight distribution and App Store submission into a standalone job running on Ubuntu, avoiding Python environment issues on macOS runners. Post-upload actions now run even when builds are skipped because an existing binary is already available, enabling reliable retry and replay behavior. Android promotion logic was aligned with the same success-or-skipped flow so production promotion can proceed without requiring a rebuild.

items.title: Revamp open-subsite dialog layout in Nexior 🎨
items.summary: The open-subsite dialog has been visually improved for better user experience. Key changes include centering the dialog in the viewport and adjusting button sizes for a more balanced appearance. The layout now features comfortable spacing between buttons, enhancing clarity and usability.

items.title: Version 3.252.1 Ready for Automated Mobile Release Validation in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: Updated Nexior to version 3.252.1 with versionCode 55201 across package metadata, Android, iOS, and shared mobile constants. The change synchronizes previously stale native version values and prepares a fresh release candidate for end-to-end testing of the new automated mobile release pipeline. This enables validation of Android promotion workflows and iOS external distribution and review submission paths using a new version identifier.

items.title: Near-fully automated Android and iOS release pipeline in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a near-fully automated mobile release process for Nexior. Android releases now default to beta testing with an optional automatic promotion to production, including staged rollouts. iOS builds automatically distribute to external TestFlight groups and submit for App Store review, with fail-safe handling for manual fixes. A new one-click workflow orchestrates both platforms, reducing manual steps while maintaining safety checks.

items.title: Refresh Studio Social Preview with Real Brand & Model Logos in Nexior ✨
items.summary: Updated the Open Graph preview for AceData Studio to replace the old stylized 'Ace' mark with the real brand logo and six visually distinct AI model logos. Removed version numbers to prevent stale cards and applied the brand teal palette for consistency. Added a new HTML artifact to enable future screenshot updates via Playwright. Modified `index.html` and `seo.ts` to point to the new preview image, ensuring immediate updates for X link previews.

items.title: Inject Stripe Key for Android Build in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update wires the `VITE_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY` secret into the Android web build process, ensuring that it is available in production bundles. The change modifies the build workflow to include the necessary environment variable, allowing the Stripe PaymentSheet to initialize correctly without errors. Overall, this is a low-risk CI change that enhances payment functionality on Android.

items.title: Enhance WeChat Login Experience for Mobile Users in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a helpful hint for mobile users attempting to log in via WeChat. Users are now prompted to screenshot the QR code and access it through WeChat's album for a seamless login experience. This change improves user guidance without requiring any backend modifications or native code adjustments.

items.title: Android Stripe PaymentSheet Lands for In-App Checkout in Nexior 💳
items.summary: Android checkout now uses Stripe’s native PaymentSheet instead of opening a browser PaymentLink, enabling in-app card entry and 3DS flows. The update adds the Capacitor Stripe plugin, routes Android Stripe payments through PaymentIntent client secrets, and preserves existing iOS and web payment behavior. Release documentation was expanded with Android Stripe setup and required publishable key configuration.

items.title: Route Android Stripe orders through PaymentIntent for native PaymentSheet in Backend ⚡
items.summary: This update changes the Stripe payment flow for Android clients to use PaymentIntents instead of PaymentLinks, enabling native in-app checkout with PaymentSheet. The `client_secret` from the new PayBackend endpoint is now stored in `order.metadata.stripe_client_secret`, while other surfaces remain on the existing flow. No database migrations are required, and iOS and web flows remain unchanged. Key helper and view logic were added to support this Android-specific path.

items.title: Added Stripe PaymentSheet Support via PaymentIntent Endpoint in Pay Backend 💳
items.summary: Introduced a new POST /payment/stripe/intent endpoint that creates Stripe PaymentIntents and returns a client_secret for native mobile PaymentSheet integrations. Updated payment status lookup to route by ID prefix, allowing the same GET endpoint to support both PaymentIntent and existing PaymentLink flows. Existing web-based PaymentLink behavior remains unchanged, enabling Android in-app payments without affecting current integrations.

items.title: Upgrade lint-staged to 17.0.5 in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the lint-staged package from version 16.4.0 to 17.0.5, introducing several important fixes and improvements. Key changes include better handling of long lists of staged files and enhancements to task modifications during commits. This upgrade ensures smoother linting processes and improved compatibility with newer Node.js versions.
